name: Hanoi
description: Vietnam's ancient capital is a city of contradictions — French cafés next to communist monuments, electric motorbike swarms around thousand-year-old temples, and the world's best bowl of pho served at plastic stools at 6am.

quickInfo.visa: E-visa 90 days, multiple entry. Free for some nationalities under bilateral agreements.

hotels.name: Little Charm Hanoi Hostel
hotels.description: Charming family-run hostel in the heart of Hanoi's Old Quarter, rooftop terrace

hotels.name: La Siesta Premium Hang Be
hotels.description: Award-winning boutique hotel with elegant Vietnamese design, rooftop pool and spa

hotels.name: Sofitel Legend Metropole Hanoi
hotels.description: Vietnam's most storied hotel since 1901 — Ho Chi Minh, Graham Greene, and Charlie Chaplin all slept here

hotels.name: InterContinental Hanoi Landmark72
hotels.description: Vietnam's tallest building (72 floors) with panoramic city views and rooftop bar

hotels.name: Hanoi La Castela Hotel
hotels.description: Stylish French-influenced boutique hotel steps from Hoan Kiem Lake

activities.name: Ha Long Bay 2D/1N Cruise
activities.description: Sail through UNESCO-listed limestone karsts, kayak sea caves, and sleep on a junk boat

activities.name: Hanoi Old Quarter Street Food Walk
activities.description: Eat bun cha, bahn cuon, nem ran, and egg coffee at the locals' favorite spots

activities.name: Hanoi City Highlights Cyclo & Walking Tour
activities.description: Cyclo ride through the 36 Old Quarter streets, Ho Chi Minh Mausoleum, Temple of Literature

activities.name: Ninh Binh Tam Coc Boat Tour
activities.description: Row boat through rice paddy caves — 'Halong Bay on land' — plus ancient temples

activities.name: Vietnamese Cooking Class in Old Quarter
activities.description: Market shopping + cook 4 authentic Northern Vietnamese dishes including fresh pho

items.activity: Pho bo breakfast on a plastic stool in the Old Quarter
Hoan Kiem Lake morning walk & Ngoc Son Temple
Old Quarter exploration — 36 traditional trade streets
Temple of Literature — Vietnam's first university (1070)
Ho Chi Minh Mausoleum complex & One Pillar Pagoda
Beer corner (Ta Hien Street) with bia hoi draft beer
Minibus transfer to Ha Long Bay pier (3.5h)
Board luxury junk boat, lunch on board
Squid fishing off the back of the boat
Tai chi on the bow deck at sunrise
Breakfast on board, explore final cave
Back in Hanoi: Old Quarter food walk
Hom Market: silk and handicraft shopping
Farewell dinner: Vietnamese BBQ & cha ca fish

practicalInfo.gettingThere: Noi Bai International Airport (HAN) — 45 min from city. Airport bus #86 to Old Quarter: 35 min, $1. Grab car to Old Quarter: $8-12. Taxi by meter recommended (avoid unmarked taxis).
practicalInfo.localTransport: Grab is essential. Cyclos (cycle rickshaws) for tourist sightseeing $5-10/hour. Xe om motorbike taxis for short hops. Old Quarter very walkable. City bus lines exist but complex.
